title: New York: no general deletion right yet
summary: New York has not enacted a comprehensive consumer-privacy law with a general right to delete (the SHIELD Act covers data-breach security, not deletion). A broader New York privacy bill has been proposed but is not in force. To remove your information, rely on the data broker's own opt-out (below) and any federal or sector-specific routes that apply.
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. New York law here is limited; the broker's own opt-out (below) is the practical route — verify before relying on it.
